prose:
**Ferrocene fukui-reactivity descriptor** — GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical tight-binding), single-point (no geometry optimization) on the supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz` geometry, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Partial charges (Mulliken scheme) were computed for the neutral, cation (+1, mult 2), and anion (−1, mult 2) states to derive the Fukui+ and Fukui− indices. Software: xtb (GFN2-xTB) via chemkit CLI invocation:
```
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz \
  --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 --out ferrocene_xtb_fukui.json --stdout path
```
Convergence: SCF converged; integrity.status ok, trustworthy true. Warnings: none reported. Result: the most electrophilic site is atom 11 (Fe) with Fukui+ = 0.22885456; the most nucleophilic site is also atom 11 (Fe) with Fukui− = 0.25938191. Output JSON: `ferrocene_xtb_fukui.json` (contains full per-atom fukui+, fukui−, fukui⁰, dual-descriptor arrays).
